message:
feat(linalg): add CASP solver portfolio and conformal calibration for adaptive solver selection

Introduce the Condition-Aware Solver Portfolio (CASP), a Bayesian
decision-theoretic system for automatically selecting the optimal
linear algebra solver based on matrix conditioning. This replaces the
previous one-size-fits-all LU decomposition approach with adaptive
solver dispatch.

fsci-runtime — CASP subsystem:
- MatrixConditionState: 4-state classification (WellConditioned,
  Moderate, IllConditioned, NearSingular) based on reciprocal
  condition number thresholds
- SolverAction: 5 solver strategies (DirectLU, PivotedQR,
  SVDFallback, DiagonalFastPath, TriangularFastPath)
- SolverPortfolio: expected-loss minimization engine with a 5x4
  loss matrix that selects the solver minimizing expected cost
  given the observed condition state
- SolverEvidenceEntry: audit trail recording solver decisions
  and observed backward errors for retrospective analysis
- ConformalCalibrator: safety net that tracks nonconformity scores
  (backward errors) and triggers SVD fallback when empirical
  miscoverage exceeds the target rate (alpha + epsilon)

fsci-linalg — solver implementations and optimizations:
- Replace O(n^3) SVD-based condition number estimation with
  fast_rcond_from_lu() — an O(n) reciprocal condition estimate
  from LU diagonal elements
- Add solve_qr() (QR decomposition path) and solve_svd_fallback()
  (SVD-based solve with full decomposition) as alternative strategies
- Add solve_with_casp() which queries the portfolio, dispatches to
  the selected solver, and records evidence for the audit trail
- Add compute_backward_error() for ||Ax - b|| / (||A|| ||x|| + ||b||)
  with backward_error: Option<f64> on SolveResult
- Optimize inv() into inv_general() (single LU factorization + solve
  against identity, O(n^3)) and inv_column_by_column() fallback
- Add randomized_rcond_estimate() using power iteration + inverse
  power iteration — O(n^2 * iters) vs O(n^3) for full SVD
- Fix lstsq residuals: only computed when rows > cols AND rank == cols
  (matches SciPy behavior)
- Add proptest property-based tests for roundtrip solve, inverse
  roundtrip, and det-inverse consistency

Workspace: add proptest, criterion, and rand as workspace dependencies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
